phase_1.audio: Acoustic analysis reveals baseline of 48.8 dBA with periodic spikes up to 69.8 dBA throughout the 289s recording. This quiet setting is favorable for sustained focus and deep work.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 61 bpm as session average. This deviates from ideal focus-state parameters.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) diverges from kinetic patterns. Acoustic consistency observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session exemplifies near-optimal conditions: minimal acoustic disturbance (56.8 dB), variable positioning, and strong focus indicators. Duration (289s) borderline for sustained concentration assessment.

phase_3.recommendations: Current 289s sessions reasonable; consider testing longer 30-min blocks. Practice deliberate stillness; reduce repositioning frequency during study periods.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (56.8dB), user-state stability (active), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (289s).
